title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to digital design methodology using Verilog HDL -
Design flow - Modeling abstraction levels, gate level model, RTL
model, behavioral model - Simulation and synthesis - Modeling for
ASIC/FPGA - Language concepts - Data types and operators -
Structural, data flow and behavioral models - Hierarchical structure
- Combinational and sequential circuit description - Continuous and
procedural assignments - Blocking and non-blocking assignments -
Delay modeling - Parameterized reusable design– Compiler
directives - Testbenches.

title: Module 2
content:
Combinational Modules, Modelling and simulation using
Verilog: Adders Half adder, Full adder, ripple carry adder),
comparators, Decoders, Encoders, Multiplexers, Demultiplexers.
Sequential modules Modelling and simulation using Verilog:
Flip flop (JK, D), Shift registers (SISO, SIPO, PISO, PIPO,
Universal shift Register), synchronous Counters (up, down,
Up/down using mode control, Mod-N).

title: Module 3
content:
Timing characteristics and Impediments to synchronous design
of sequential networks – setup time – hold time – propagation
delay –- maximum clock frequency- clock skew. Analysis of
synchronous sequential circuits - Derivation of state graphs and
state tables, General model of sequential networks. Design of
synchronous sequential circuits - Finite state machines - Mealy and
Moore models - State graphs - State assignment - State table - State
reduction- Design of Sequence Detector

title: Module 4
content:
Programmable LSI techniques -PLA/PAL/PLDs - Building Logic
Functions
with
PLA/PAL,
Read-Only
Memory
(ROM)-
Combinational Circuit Implementation with ROM- Sequential
Circuit Implementation with ROM CPLD and FPGAs -
Xilinx/Altera series FPGAs - Programmable System on Chip - Zynq
So design overview.
